steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence uses an inverted structure: "So shrewd an observer is Shonagon... that her book's musings on tenth-century Japanese courtly life ______." Strip the prepositional phrase: the true subject is 'musings' (plural), so the verb must agree with a plural noun.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "fascinate": plural present tense — agrees with the plural subject 'musings.' ✓
  • "has fascinated": 'has' marks singular present perfect — 'musings has' violates subject-verb agreement. ✗
  • "is fascinating": 'is' marks singular present progressive — 'musings is' violates subject-verb agreement. ✗
  • "fascinates": the -s ending marks singular present tense — 'musings fascinates' violates subject-verb agreement. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
